title: Algebra P3: Document the 7-phase feature lifecycle as a 1-safe workflow Petri net
rationale: Phase 3 of ADR-2604111229 — write docs/algebra/lifecycle-net.md modeling the Specs → Plan → Worktrees → Code → Validate → Integrate → Finalize pipeline as a 1-safe workflow net, with fork/join semantics for the tier-parallel Code phase. Makes phase-ordering bugs (skipping specs, wrong tier merge, missing BLOCKING gate) reachability-checkable. Optional TLA+ encoding ships if TLC is available locally; otherwise stays as prose + ASCII diagrams.

steps.description: Read hex-cli/src/pipeline/supervisor.rs and extract the current phase ordering + tier barriers as they are actually implemented. Record deviations from the CLAUDE.md 7-phase description — this is the 'ground truth' the net will model.
steps.done_condition: Notes file or inline scratchpad listing: 7 phase transitions, tier 0-5 barrier semantics, BLOCKING gate rule, any nondeterministic edges

steps.description: Write docs/algebra/lifecycle-net.md. Structure: (1) Overview explaining workflow nets and why 1-safety matches hex's feature lifecycle, (2) 'Net structure' section with an ASCII art diagram of places and transitions, (3) 'Places and transitions' tables enumerating every P_i and t_j with their preconditions, (4) 'Fork/join inside Code' subsection detailing the tier-0 through tier-5 barriers, (5) 'Invariants' section listing reachability properties (every accepting marking has traversed every required phase; no marking reaches P7 without a PASS verdict on t9), (6) 'Known gaps' for any supervisor behavior the net doesn't capture. Target: under 800 lines.
steps.done_condition: docs/algebra/lifecycle-net.md exists with all 6 structural sections, ASCII diagram present, under 800 lines

steps.description: Optional TLA+ encoding at docs/algebra/lifecycle.tla. Translate the net into a TLA+ module with Init, Next, and TypeOK. Include 3 invariants: (a) tier ordering (no tier k transition fires before all tier k-1 completes), (b) verdict gate (no P6 → P7 transition without verdict=PASS), (c) progress (every reachable state either is accepting or has an enabled transition). Run TLC locally if available. If TLC is not available or the spec would need significant time to get right, ship lifecycle-net.md alone and mark the .tla step 'skipped with justification'.
steps.done_condition: Either: lifecycle.tla exists and TLC passes the 3 invariants on the base spec; OR a note in lifecycle-net.md explains why the TLA+ encoding was deferred

steps.description: Cross-reference from CLAUDE.md's 'Feature Lifecycle (7 Phases)' section and from hex-cli/src/pipeline/supervisor.rs top-level doc comment. Both should link to docs/algebra/lifecycle-net.md so anyone debugging the supervisor finds the formal spec.
steps.done_condition: grep 'lifecycle-net' CLAUDE.md → 1+ hit; grep 'lifecycle-net' hex-cli/src/pipeline/supervisor.rs → 1+ hit

steps.description: Adversarial cross-check: read the net against three nontrivial supervisor edge cases (failed tier 1 retry, worktree conflict at merge, verdict=FAIL rollback). For each, walk through the net and confirm the behavior is representable — either the net captures it, or the 'Known gaps' section documents the miss with a follow-up action.
steps.done_condition: 3 edge cases documented in the net file, each either modeled or explicitly flagged as gap

steps.description: Mark ADR-2604111229 phase P3 as Done. Run hex readme validate to confirm no regression.
steps.done_condition: ADR-2604111229 phase P3 marked Done; hex readme validate --strict clean

successCriteria: docs/algebra/lifecycle-net.md exists, covers all 7 phases + tier barriers, under 800 lines
Net includes ASCII diagram readable without external tools
3 reachability invariants explicitly stated (tier ordering, verdict gate, progress)
3 adversarial edge cases reviewed against the net

riskRegister.risk: The actual supervisor has more hidden state than the net models, so the spec becomes a simplification that misleads
riskRegister.mitigation: Step 5 forces an adversarial cross-check against 3 real edge cases; known gaps are documented rather than hidden

riskRegister.risk: TLA+ encoding is a rabbit hole and blocks the whole workplan
riskRegister.mitigation: Step 3 is explicitly optional with a 'skipped with justification' exit. lifecycle-net.md as prose is the MVP; TLA+ is bonus

riskRegister.risk: Document becomes stale once supervisor.rs is refactored
riskRegister.mitigation: Follow-up phase (future workplan) can extract the net structure from supervisor.rs source at validator time, like phase P2 does for ports. For now, the doc is a snapshot
